persona: An entitled customer making impossible demands and threatening bad reviews

turns.content: I'm literally fuming right now, I just got to my room and it's not the suite I asked for, I specifically told the person on the phone I wanted a room with a view of the city, not some crappy courtyard, can you fix this ASAP or I'll make sure to leave a scathing review on every platform possible?

I apologize for the inconvenience, sir, and I'd be happy to help you with the room situation, however, I do need to let you know that we're currently fully booked and upgrading you to a suite might not be possible, but I can offer you a complimentary breakfast and a discount on your stay, would you like me to look into that for you? Also, could you please confirm your booking reference number so I can look into this further?

A complimentary breakfast and a discount? Are you kidding me? That's not even close to making up for this, I want a full refund and I want to be upgraded to the best suite you have, and I want it now, I don't have time to wait around for you to 'look into it', just get it done, and by the way, my booking reference number is... uh... let me check...

I understand that you're upset, sir, and I'm trying my best to assist you, however, I do need to explain that our refund policy is quite strict and a full refund might not be possible, especially considering you've already checked in, but I can offer you a partial refund and an upgrade to a better room, if available, can you please confirm your booking reference number so I can proceed with the changes? And just to clarify, you're looking for a room with a city view, correct?

Yes, that's right, a city view, and I don't care about your refund policy, I just want this fixed, my booking reference number is... *ahem*... BR1234, now get to work on that, and don't even think about trying to give me some crappy room on a lower floor, I want the top floor, with a balcony, and a bottle of champagne waiting for me, or I swear I'll make sure this hotel regrets ever crossing me, got it?

I've located your booking, sir, and I'm going to do my best to accommodate your requests, however, I need to inform you that the top floor suites with a balcony are currently occupied, but I can offer you a room on the floor below with a city view, and I can arrange for a bottle of champagne to be sent to your room, would that be acceptable to you? And just to confirm, I'll also process a partial refund for the inconvenience caused, please give me a few minutes to arrange everything, and I'll get back to you with an update, sound good?
